english: pf. bsad (bsad), fut. (Lex. gsad (gsad), usually:) bsad (bsad), imp. sod (sod), W. *sád-če* (*sad che*), 1. to kill, slay, murder, slaughter. gsód-pa-la dgá-ba (gsod pa la dga' ba) to delight in killing Dzl.; bsad ma-t́ág-pai śa (bsad ma thag pa'i sha), gsár-du bsád-pai śa (gsar du bsad pa'i sha) v. gsár-ba (gsar ba); rgyál-pos gsód-pa (rgyal pos gsod pa) to be executed by the authority Dzl.; (tśe) bsád-pa=la t́úg-nas ((tshe) bsad pa=la thug nas) when he was just on the point of being executed Dzl.; *pag j̇áb-te sád-tan-če* (*pag jab te sad tan che*) W. to assassinate; bsád-do (bsad do)! bcóm-mo (bcom mo)! I am lost! it is all over with me! Wdn. — 2. to put out, extinguish frq. — 3. kyád-gsod-pa (kyad gsod pa) to despise, v. kyad (kyad) extr. Comp. gsod-gcód (gsod gcod) the act of murdering, murder, slaughter, gsod-gcód-kyi jı́gs-pa (gsod gcod kyi 'jigs pa) fear of murder Mil.; gsod-gcód máṅ-po byed (gsod gcod mang po byed) he is murdering, slaughtering, a great deal. — gsod-byéd (gsod byed) killer, murderer, gsod-byéd rṅán-pa (gsod byed rngan pa) the murderous huntsman Lex.; gsod-byéd-kyi gnas (gsod byed kyi gnas) slaughter-house Stg. — gsod-sa (gsod sa) place of execution Thgy. — gsód-lugs (gsod lugs) way of killing, sṅar ma byas (sngar ma byas) a new (way of killing) Tar.

I. A. vb., 1. intrs. to live, to be alive, gsón-no (gson no) he (she etc.) is alive, *de méd-na mi són-če mi túb* (*de med na mi son che mi thub*) without that a man cannot live W.; to remain alive, to save or preserve one's own life, rdzún-du smrás-na gson kyaṅ (rdzun du smras na gson kyang) though I could save my life by (telling) a falsehood Dzl.; gsón-par mi 。dod (gson par mi 'dod) I do not wish to live (any longer) Dzl.; of the fire: to burn, *da són-na* (*da son na*) W. does it burn now? — 2. trs. to wake, to rouse from sleep by shaking, to urge on, to hurry on (lazy people), by force, whereas skúl-ba (skul ba) is only done by words. — B. sbst. life, gsón-pai tśe-na (gson pa'i tshe na) or gsón-pai dús-su (gson pa'i dus su) during (my, your etc.) life, in lifetime, frq.; hence: gson-pa-nyid (gson pa nyid) for ζωή John 1, 4 etc. Chr. P. — C. adj. living, alive, frq. gsón-po (gson po), col. *gsón-te* (*gson te*), kyé-ma bu gsón-po mt́óṅ-ṅo (kye ma bu gson po mthong ngo) ah, there I see my son again alive! Stg.; gsón-por byéd-pa (gson por byed pa) to call into life, to animate, gsón-por dúr-du júg-pa (gson por dur du 'jug pa) to bury alive Dzl., gsón-pai rgyú-ma 。drén-pa (gson pa'i rgyu ma 'dren pa) to tear out the bowels of a living man; *son-te* (*son te*) W. also: healthy, whole, restored to health again, *kaṅ-pa, mig, me són-te čó-če* (*kang pa, mig, me son te cho che*) to cure a foot, an eye, to blow a fire into flame again; entire, whole, undivided, *són-te kyóṅ-ṅa* (*son te kyong nga*) am I to bring it entire (or cut into pieces etc.)? of the moon: full. — gsón-ma (gson ma) rarely for gsón-po (gson po), ṕúg-ron gsón-ma (phug ron gson ma) a live pigeon Pth.; gson-gšin (gson gshin) both the living and the dead Cs.; gson bsrégs (gson bsregs) a creature burnt alive, mi žig(-la) gson-bsrégs byéd-pa (mi zhig(-la) gson bsregs byed pa) to burn a person alive Pth. II. vb., pf. bsan (bsan), fut. gsan (gsan), imp. gson (gson) Cs., prob. the original form of gsán-pa (gsan pa) to hear. III. čud-gson-pa (chud gson pa) v. čud (chud).

pf. bsab (bsab), fut. gsab (gsab), 1. to fill out or up, to supply, complete, make up, hór-koṅs (hor kongs) a gap (?) Sch., to cure, wounds W., *sob-mán* (*sob man*) balm, ointment for wounds. — 2. to pay, repay, return, skyín-pa (skyin pa) a loan Sch., drin gsób-pa (drin gsob pa) to return a kindness Glr.
1. v. sór (sor) I. — 2. supine of gsó-ba (gso ba).
to brandish, flourish, a staff Cs.

I. vb., 1. = žú-ba (zhu ba) 1, q.v.; rgyál-po-la sróg-gi skyabs gsól-to (rgyal po la srog gi skyabs gsol to) he besought the king to save his life Dzl.; stón-pa-la smón-lam btáb-par gsol čig (ston pa la smon lam btab par gsol chig) ask the teacher to say the prayers Dzl.; bstán-du gsol (bstan du gsol) I beg to explain, frq.; lha gsól-ba (lha gsol ba) to worship a god, by offerings, libations etc. Glr. and elsewh. Tibetans when arriving on the top of a mountain-pass generally mutter the words: gsól-lo gsól-lo (gsol lo gsol lo), prob. to express their thankfulness for having been preserved from harm so far, and to implore further protection. — 2. resp. for gón-pa (gon pa) and skón-pa (skon pa) to put on: (skú-la) ná-bza (sku la na bza), čós-gos (chos gos), dbú-la t́od (dbu la thod), žábs-la čag (zhabs la chag) (to put on) a garment, clerical robes, cap, shoes Dzl., Glr.; slób-dpon-la ber-čén skú-la gsól-te (slob dpon la ber chen sku la gsol te) putting the cloak on the teacher Ma.; for zá-ba (za ba), t́uṅ-ba (thung ba), also for 。drén-pa ('dren pa), to eat, to drink, to offer a meal Dzl.; rgyál-po 。bras-čáṅ gsól-nas (rgyal po 'bras chang gsol nas) as the king had drunk rice-wine Glr.; to take, to give, administer (medicine) Dzl.; to place (food etc.) before, to
